description:
You are acting as the on-call database reliability engineer.  
    A nightly job has dumped several new backup archives into the directory  

        /home/user/db_backups  

    and you must move every file whose name ends in “.sql.gz” into the already-existing sub-directory  

        /home/user/db_backups/archive  

    After the move you must append *one* line to the log file  

        /home/user/db_backups/backup_move.log  

    The automated grader will verify three things:  

    1.  All of the files that originally matched “*.sql.gz” in  
       /home/user/db_backups/ are now located inside  
       /home/user/db_backups/archive/ and **no longer** exist in the
       parent directory.  
    2.  Exactly one new line has been appended (not overwritten) to
       /home/user/db_backups/backup_move.log.  
    3.  That new line follows **precisely** this format (UTC time, one
       blank space, literal word “moved”, one blank space, integer count,
       one blank space, literal word “files”):  

           YYYY-MM-DD_HH:MM:SS moved N files  

       • The timestamp must match the output of  
         `date -u "+%F_%T"` at the moment the command is executed.  
       • N must equal the exact number of “*.sql.gz” files that were
         moved.  For the initial state supplied to you, the correct value of
         N is 3.  

    IMPORTANT OPERATING RULES  
    • Perform the entire action with **one single shell command line**.  
      (Using operators such as “&&”, “;”, pipes, command substitution,
      etc., is fine—as long as it is issued as a single line.)  
    • Do not remove, modify or rename any file that does **not** end in
      “.sql.gz”.  
    • Do not create any additional files other than the required log-file
      entry.  
    • The grader will call `grep -E "^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}_[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} moved [0-9]+ files$"` on the
      last line of /home/user/db_backups/backup_move.log, so the format
      **must be exact**.  

    Recap of current contents (for your reference—do **not**
    hard-code these names in the log):

        /home/user/db_backups/
            prod_full_2023-12-31.sql.gz
            prod_inc_2024-01-01.sql.gz
            prod_inc_2024-01-02.sql.gz
            readme.txt
            backup_move.log
            archive/                 (directory)

    Goal: after your single command, the three “*.sql.gz” files shown
    above must live inside /home/user/db_backups/archive/, the parent
    directory must no longer contain any “*.sql.gz” files, and
    backup_move.log must have one correctly-formatted new line describing
    the move.
truth:
Initial file structure before the agent begins:

    /home/user/db_backups/
        archive/
            prod_full_2023-12-30.sql.gz                (size / content irrelevant)
        prod_full_2023-12-31.sql.gz                    (placeholder text)
        prod_inc_2024-01-01.sql.gz                     (placeholder text)
        prod_inc_2024-01-02.sql.gz                     (placeholder text)
        readme.txt                                     (contains: "Backups are dumped nightly.\n")
        backup_move.log                                (contents EXACTLY):
            2024-02-15_10:00:00 moved 2 files
            2024-02-16_10:00:00 moved 1 files

    Verification after agent runs:

    1. Files prod_full_2023-12-31.sql.gz,
       prod_inc_2024-01-01.sql.gz and
       prod_inc_2024-01-02.sql.gz must be located inside
       /home/user/db_backups/archive/ and no longer exist directly in
       /home/user/db_backups/.

    2. /home/user/db_backups/backup_move.log must now have **three** lines;
       the third (last) line must match the regular expression  
       ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}_[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} moved 3 files$

    3. No other changes to the filesystem are expected.
name: task_000000_f8abab22
